questions.question: Which CSS method is best for overall page layout structure?
questions.explanation: CSS Grid handles the two-dimensional page structure (rows and columns simultaneously), making it ideal for overall page layout.

questions.question: What property defines grid area placements in a template?
questions.explanation: grid-template-areas defines named grid areas using ASCII-art-like strings that map to HTML elements.

questions.question: What is the Holy Grail layout?
options: Header, nav, main, aside, and footer sections
questions.explanation: The Holy Grail layout consists of a full-width header, three columns (nav, main, aside), and a full-width footer.

questions.question: Which unit is best for flexible column sizing in a layout?
questions.explanation: The fr (fraction) unit distributes available space proportionally in CSS Grid tracks, unlike percentages which include gaps.

questions.question: What CSS property ensures a layout fills the full viewport height?
questions.explanation: min-height: 100vh ensures the layout container is at least as tall as the viewport, enabling the sticky footer pattern.

questions.question: What is the recommended approach for combining Grid and Flexbox in a website layout?
options: Use Grid for page structure, Flexbox for components
questions.explanation: The macro/micro approach uses Grid for the page skeleton (macro) and Flexbox for component-level layouts (micro) like navigation and cards.

questions.question: How do you create a sticky footer with CSS Grid?
questions.explanation: grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto with min-height: 100vh makes the middle row expand to fill space, pushing the footer down.

questions.question: What is required for position: sticky to work on a sidebar?
options: An explicit top, bottom, left, or right value
questions.explanation: position: sticky requires at least one offset property (top, bottom, left, right) to define the threshold where it becomes fixed.

questions.question: Which responsive grid pattern creates columns that adapt without media queries?
questions.explanation: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(250px, 1fr)) creates as many columns as fit with a minimum of 250px, adapting automatically to viewport width.

questions.question: What is a common mistake when using Flexbox for page layout?
options: Flexbox is one-dimensional and struggles with two-dimensional page structure
questions.explanation: Flexbox is one-dimensional (row OR column). For page layouts with rows AND columns, CSS Grid is the better choice.

questions.question: What does the grid-template-areas value '.' (dot) represent?
options: An empty cell in the grid
questions.explanation: A dot (.) in grid-template-areas represents an empty cell where no element is placed.

questions.question: How can you ensure a main content area has a maximum width for readability?
questions.explanation: max-width with margin: 0 auto ensures the content is centered and doesn't exceed optimal line length for readability.

questions.question: Which semantic HTML elements should replace generic divs for layout regions?
questions.explanation: Semantic HTML5 elements provide landmarks for screen reader navigation and improve SEO. They describe the purpose of each region.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the contain: layout property in layout components?
options: Prevents layout recalculations from propagating outside the container
questions.explanation: contain: layout isolates the component's layout so changes inside don't cause recalculations outside, improving performance.

questions.question: What does the auto-fill keyword in grid-template-columns do?
options: Creates as many tracks as fit, keeping empty tracks
Creates as many tracks as fit, collapsing empty ones
questions.explanation: auto-fill creates grid tracks even if there aren't enough items to fill them. auto-fit collapses empty tracks.

questions.question: How should layout regions be ordered for accessibility when using CSS Grid?
options: Visual order can differ from DOM order, but semantic reading order matters
Visual order must match DOM order exactly
questions.explanation: CSS Grid placement changes visual order but not DOM order. Screen readers follow DOM order, so maintain logical DOM order.

questions.question: What is a dashboard layout pattern in CSS Grid?
questions.explanation: A dashboard layout typically has a sidebar (often sticky) on the left, a top header, and a main content area, sometimes with a right panel.

questions.question: What does the clamp() function enable in responsive layouts?
options: Fluid typography that scales between min and max without media queries
questions.explanation: clamp(min, preferred, max) enables fluid typography and sizing that scales between defined minimum and maximum values.

questions.question: What is the correct way to center a grid item within its cell?
options: place-items: center on the grid container
questions.explanation: place-items: center is shorthand for align-items: center and justify-items: center, centering items in their grid cells.

questions.question: Why should you use gap instead of margins for spacing in grid and flex layouts?
options: gap prevents margin collapsing and double spacing
gap is the only supported method
questions.explanation: gap provides consistent spacing between items without margin collapsing issues or the need to remove margins from first/last items.
